From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

## Page version status

This is an accepted version of this page

This is the [latest accepted revision](/wiki/Wikipedia:Pending_changes "Wikipedia:Pending changes"), [reviewed](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Special:Log&type=review&page=Middleweight) on *11 March 2026*.

Weight class in combat sports

**Middleweight** is a [weight class](/wiki/Weight_class "Weight class") in [combat sports](/wiki/Combat_sport "Combat sport").

## Boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Middleweight&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Boxing")]

### Professional

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Middleweight&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Professional")]

In [professional boxing](/wiki/Professional_boxing "Professional boxing"), the middleweight division is contested above 154 lb (70 kg) and up to 160 lb (73 kg).

Early boxing history is less than exact, but the middleweight designation seems to have begun in the 1840s. In the [bare-knuckle](/wiki/Bare-knuckle_boxing "Bare-knuckle boxing") era, the first middleweight championship fight was between Tom Chandler and Dooney Harris in 1867. Chandler won, becoming known as the American middleweight champion. The first middleweight fight with gloves *may* have been between George Fulljames and [Jack (Nonpareil) Dempsey](/wiki/Jack_(Nonpareil)_Dempsey "Jack (Nonpareil) Dempsey") (no relation to the more famous [heavyweight](/wiki/Heavyweight "Heavyweight") [Jack Dempsey](/wiki/Jack_Dempsey "Jack Dempsey")).

## Kickboxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Middleweight&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Kickboxing")]

* [International Kickboxing Federation](/wiki/International_Kickboxing_Federation "International Kickboxing Federation") (IKF) Middleweight (Pro & Amateur) 159.1–165 lb. or 72.4–75 kg
* In [Glory](/wiki/Glory_(kickboxing) "Glory (kickboxing)") promotion, a middleweight division is up to 85 kg (187 lb).[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* In [Bellator Kickboxing](/wiki/Bellator_Kickboxing "Bellator Kickboxing") promotion, a middleweight division is up to 85 kg (187 lb).
* In [ONE Championship](/wiki/ONE_Championship "ONE Championship"), the middleweight division limit is 93 kg (205 lb).

## Bare-knuckle boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Middleweight&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Bare-knuckle boxing")]

The limit for middleweight generally differs among promotions in bare-knuckle boxing:

* In [Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship"), the middleweight division has an upper limit of 175 lb (79 kg).
* In [BYB Extreme Fighting Series](/wiki/BYB_Extreme_Fighting_Series "BYB Extreme Fighting Series"), the middleweight division has an upper limit of 160 lb (73 kg).
* In BKB™, the middleweight division has an upper limit of 89 kg (196 lb).

## Lethwei

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Middleweight&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Lethwei")]

The [World Lethwei Championship](/wiki/World_Lethwei_Championship "World Lethwei Championship") recognizes the middleweight division with an upper limit of 75 kg (165 lb). In [World Lethwei Championship](/wiki/World_Lethwei_Championship "World Lethwei Championship"), [Naimjon Tuhtaboyev](/wiki/Naimjon_Tuhtaboyev "Naimjon Tuhtaboyev") is the Middleweight Champion.

## Mixed Martial Arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Middleweight&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Mixed Martial Arts")]

Main article: [Middleweight (MMA)](/wiki/Middleweight_(MMA) "Middleweight (MMA)")

In [MMA](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"), the middleweight division is from 171 lb (77.5 kg) to 185 lb (84 kg).

[Anderson Silva](/wiki/Anderson_Silva "Anderson Silva") is considered to be the greatest Middleweight [MMA](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") fighter of all time.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

| Middleweight | 76–83 kg | 65–70 kg | [World Championships](/wiki/World_Taekwondo_Championships "World Taekwondo Championships"), Continental Championships, [Asian Games](/wiki/Taekwondo_at_the_2006_Asian_Games "Taekwondo at the 2006 Asian Games") | until 1998 |
